narration_text: Now we'll see how packet queueing delay is affected by the traffic intensity, which is essentially the ratio of the average packet arrival rate to the link bandwidth. Notice that as this ratio approaches one, the average queueing delay increases significantly. Look at the image on the slide, observe how the delay changes as the traffic intensity varies. This tells us that when the arrival rate of bits exceeds the service rate, the queue will keep growing, leading to an infinite delay. I want you to focus on the concept of traffic intensity and its impact on queueing delay, as this will be crucial in understanding network performance, and we'll explore more of this in the next section.
